summary: A logarithm answers the question 'what exponent?': log_b(x) is the power you raise b to in order to get x. The laws of logarithms turn products, quotients and powers inside a log into sums, differences and multiples — the key to simplifying and to solving exponential equations.
formulas: change of base: log_b x = ln x / ln b
method: Identify products, quotients and powers inside the logarithm.
Use the product law to split a product into a sum of logs.
Use the quotient law to split a quotient into a difference of logs.
Use the power law to bring an exponent out in front as a coefficient.
To go the other way (condense), read each law from right to left.

example.title: Expand a logarithm
steps: Power law on the first term: log(x²) = 2·log(x).
